name: Breece Hall
headline: Workhorse volume, fading pass game, still an RB2 with juice
body:
Hall handled 243 carries for 1,065 yards and 4 scores across 16 games last year, and the burst that built his name is intact: a 10.7% explosive run rate, eight runs of 20-plus, and a 59-yard touchdown all point to a back who can break a game open. His 1.84 yards after contact per attempt and 12 broken tackles confirm he still creates on his own. The drag is the surrounding math, with a slightly negative EPA per play (-0.047) reflecting how little the Jets offense did to set him up.

The receiving role is the swing factor. Targets fell to 48 last season from 95 in 2023, and that passing-down work looks likely to keep eroding, which caps his PPR ceiling. Even so, the line ranks among the league's best in yards before contact and his bell-cow rushing share is secure. Draft him as a high-end RB2 and bank on volume plus explosiveness rather than the three-down monster he once profiled as.
